description: A polished designed advocacy poster. The upper portion shows a small white line-engraving of the White House and the words 'The WHITE HOUSE'. Below in a large navy serif typeface (centered) is the slogan 'HOMES ARE FOR PEOPLE, NOT CORPORATIONS.' followed by a smaller all-caps line 'PASS THE 21ST CENTURY ROAD TO HOUSING ACT'. The lower two-thirds is a warm, golden-hour color photograph of an upscale coastal/New England neighborhood: large shingled waterfront homes behind a rocky shoreline, with a tall flagpole flying a large American flag at center, calm water in the foreground. The composition is aspirational and patriotic (sunset light, flag, single-family homes). The propaganda/messaging technique is populist framing of a housing-policy push, contrasting ordinary 'people' against 'corporations' and urging passage of named legislation. Target audience: general public and would-be homeowners, promoting an administration housing initiative.
notable_text: HOMES ARE FOR PEOPLE, NOT CORPORATIONS. / PASS THE 21ST CENTURY ROAD TO HOUSING ACT
